title: Natural perfume oil roller
subtitle: A roller-ball perfume oil blend with rose, geranium, bergamot, and cedarwood
excerpt: A perfume oil roller sits at 15 to 20% total essential oil diluted in jojoba. The fragrance is more persistent than an alcohol perfume and goes on without the initial sharp top note that alcohol gives. Rose absolute, rose geranium, bergamot, and cedarwood build a floral-woody-citrus blend that develops on the skin over several hours.
sourceNotes: Perfume oil roller formulation from Tisserand Institute perfumery guide (tisserandinstitute.org, May 2026). Fragrance note structure from Arctander, Perfume and Flavor Materials of Natural Origin (1960). EO safety from Essential Oil Safety, Tisserand and Young (2014).

recipe.batchNotes: Make a small test batch first: the fragrance balance shifts over the first week as the top notes fade. Once the balance is right, scale to as many rollers as needed.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Allow the blend to macerate for 1 week before using. The fragrance integrates and develops over the first 7 days. Mark the blend date on the bottle.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 2 years. Jojoba has a very long shelf life. Rose absolute and bergamot are the most volatile components and will fade first. Keep away from direct sunlight and heat.

recipeTools.notes: Or a small beaker for blending

glossaryTerms.definition: A descriptor for where a fragrance material appears in the arc of a perfume's development on skin. Top notes are perceived first and evaporate quickly (bergamot). Heart or middle notes emerge after 10 to 20 minutes (rose geranium). Base notes anchor the blend and linger longest (cedarwood). Rose absolute sits between heart and base.

The resting period during which a perfume blend integrates and develops. During maceration, the individual essential oil components interact and the fragrance smooths into a coherent whole. 1 week is the minimum for a simple blend; longer is often better.

content.text: Perfume oils differ from alcohol-based eau de toilette in their delivery. The oil absorbs into the skin slowly, releasing fragrance over several hours rather than the quick top-note burst of an alcohol spray. The total

structure here is bergamot (top), rose geranium (heart), rose absolute (heart-base), and cedarwood (base). Bergamot is kept at 2% to avoid phototoxicity: use bergapten-free (FCF) bergamot for complete safety on the skin.
Allow the blend a minimum of 7 days of
before evaluating the fragrance. A freshly blended oil smells of its components in sequence; a week later, those edges smooth and the blend reads as a single fragrance.

items.prepNote: 3% of formula; use FCF (bergapten-free); the top note
2% of formula; rose absolute; the heart-base anchor

content.text: Combine all essential oils in a small beaker and stir briefly. Add the jojoba oil and stir to blend. Using a small funnel, decant into a 10 ml roller-ball bottle. Cap and label with the blend date. Store in a cool, dark place for at least 7 days before using. Roll onto pulse points: wrists, inner elbows, neck: and allow to dry before contact with clothing.
2 years in a cool, dark place. The bergamot top note fades first; after 1 year the blend will read as predominantly floral-woody. The base note (cedarwood) persists longest.
